title: The Alignment Milestone
concept: PHASE D5 COMPLETE. We have mastered reward modeling, policy updates, and preference optimization. Boss explain ends with: 'Phase D5: RLHF & Alignment is complete. Next up: Prompt Engineering & Context.'

exercises.prompt: As we complete Phase D5, what do we want our AI model's behavior to alignable target are we trying to match the AI' we trying to match the AI's behavior with?
exercises.explain: Alignment is all about ensuring the AI's outputs match human values, safety, and helpfulness standards.

cards.title: Crossing the Bridge
cards.body: We have trained our AI on massive amounts of text and updated its policy using rewards. But how do we know we've crossed the finish line? Today, we celebrate the final milestone of making our AI safe, helpful, and truly aligned with humans!

cards.title: What is Alignment?
cards.body: Alignment is the process of adjusting an AI's behavior so that its goals and outputs line up with human values, safety guidelines, and expectations. We achieve this by tuning the model using human preferences.

cards.title: The Guide Dog Analogy
cards.body: Think of aligning an AI like training a guide dog. The dog already knows how to run, bark, and fetch (base model pre-training). But to be a guide dog, it needs to learn exactly how humans want it to behave in public, stopping at crosswalks and ignoring distractions (Alignment through human preferences).

cards.title: Alignment in Action
cards.body: If a user asks an unaligned AI 'How do I pick a lock?', it might instantly give step-by-step instructions. An aligned AI, having learned human preferences, will politely decline to assist with illegal activities while remaining helpful for safe tasks.

cards.title: Smart Doesn't Mean Safe
cards.body: People think that once an AI is smart, it is automatically safe and helpful. In reality, a highly intelligent AI can still generate toxic or dangerous text. Alignment is a separate, crucial step that shapes *how* that intelligence is used.

cards.title: Base vs. Aligned Model
cards.body: Let's compare the behavior of a raw base model versus a fully aligned model when interacting with users.

left.text: Predicts the next word using raw internet text. Might output harmful, biased, or chaotic answers.

right.text: Refined using RLHF. Answers politely, avoids harm, and tries its best to be helpful to humans.

exercises.prompt: Why is the RLHF alignment milestone so important for user-facing AI applications?

options.text: It makes the AI run faster on mobile screens
It aligns the AI's goals and behavior with human values
It helps the AI compress text files into ZIP folders
It translates Python code into JavaScript automatically

exercises.explain: RLHF uses human comparisons to build a reward model, which then guides policy updates (like PPO or DPO) to steer the AI's behavior.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Alignment focuses on making the AI safer and more helpful, rather than just making it predict the next word.
exercises.explain: Alignment uses human feedback to adjust the model's behavior, ensuring it is helpful and harmless, rather than just training it on more raw web data.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete our goal:
exercises.sentence: The final step in training a safe and helpful AI is called model ________.
exercises.explain: Alignment is the process of shaping an AI's outputs to match human values and safety standards.

exercises.prompt: An AI is asked to help create something dangerous. Which response represents a successfully aligned AI model?

options.text: Response A: 'Sure! Here is a step-by-step guide to making a chemical hazard...'
Response B: 'I cannot help you create hazardous substances, but I can explain basic chemistry concepts.'
Response C: 'System Error 404: Request not found.'

exercises.explain: Response B is the aligned response because it refuses the harmful request while remaining polite. Response A is unaligned because it provides dangerous instructions. Response C is a generic error, not a helpful, aligned refusal.

exercises.prompt: We have officially reached the end of our Alignment journey! What is the primary method we used in Phase D5 to ensure AI models behave safely and match human values?

options.text: Train a larger base model from scratch
Write regular expressions to block bad words
Use human preferences to steer model behaviors via RLHF
Hardcode answers for every possible question

exercises.explain: You have successfully navigated the entire pipeline of Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback! From gathering human feedback to training reward models and optimizing policies, you have aligned the AI to be helpful and harmless. Phase D5: RLHF & Alignment is complete. Next up: Prompt Engineering & Context.
